title: Continuous Delivery Outcomes, Flow, Feedback, and Evidence · Guided Workflow Studio
summary: A delivery pairing session predicts each automation transition before producing the first working delivery outcome and evidence map for continuous delivery outcomes, flow, feedback, and evidence. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of delivery outcome and evidence map while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Distinguish continuous integration, delivery, deployment, release, and feature exposure by their automation, decision, and stakeholder boundaries.
Relate batch size, queue time, feedback latency, failure isolation, recovery cost, and learning speed without treating deployment frequency as the objective.
Trace requirement, source revision, review, checks, build inputs, immutable artifact, deployment, observation, and recovery evidence as one delivery chain.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# cicd-github-actions cumulative delivery-system portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic workflow, policy, artifact, and release evidence review only.
# They never call GitHub APIs, start runners, execute learner commands, access Docker or registries, exchange OIDC tokens, or contact cloud services.
workspace: cicd
version-baseline:
  checkout: 7.0.0
  setup-node: 7.0.0
  upload-artifact: 7.0.1
  download-artifact: 8.0.1
  attest: 4.1.1
  node: 24-lts
  typescript-native: 7.0.2
  typescript-api-compatibility: 6.0.2
  build-push-action: 7
  google-auth: 3
  deploy-cloudrun: 3
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-delivery-environment
evidence:

steps.title: Commit to the mission model · Workshop Ci Ci Cd Release Boundaries
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop service deploys 4 times per week but cannot connect reviewed source, required checks, immutable artifact, production revision, user signal, abort decision, or recovery. Define value and delivery evidence without gaming one metric. Competency probe: Distinguish continuous integration, delivery, deployment, release, and feature exposure by their automation, decision, and stakeholder boundaries.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Commit to the mission model · Workshop Ci Ci Cd Release Boundaries.

steps.title: Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Ci Release Hypothesis Done
steps.instruction:
Append the CI/CD evidence item headed "  # Evidence: ci-release-hypothesis-done-8i2f5a". Tailor its delivery-evidence.yaml change to the current stakeholder case, retain a deterministic workflow or release review, reject one concrete failure, and record a repair. The browser analyzes text only; run GitHub Actions, runner, Docker, registry, OIDC, Google Cloud, and deployment work later in authorized disposable transfer environments.

Evidence target: Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Ci Release Hypothesis Done.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Ci Release Hypothesis Done into observable performance evidence. For Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Ci Release Hypothesis Done, keep Ci Release Hypothesis Done connected to mechanically reviewable workflow, trust, artifact, deployment, and recovery evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: ci-release-hypothesis-done-8i2f5a
  - competency: ci-release-hypothesis-done
    artifact: delivery-evidence.yaml
    decision: release hypothesis done
    environment: reusable workflow called by three repositories at different revisions
    fault-injection: route five percent traffic to a revision with a data incompatibility
    retained-proof: artifact ID SHA-256 producer retention and verified download layout
    case-key: 8i2f5a
    change: |
      hypothesis: safer-status-release
      source-revision: reviewed-sha
      checks: required-and-observed
      artifact: immutable-digest
      deployment: revision-identity
      user-signal: successful-status-read
      abort-signal: error-budget-breach
      recovery: prior-digest
    verify: review source-to-user evidence chain and calculate lead time failure and recovery boundaries
    failure: Reject deployment count as value, green checks as provenance, or pipeline completion as user outcome.
    repair: Link reviewed source, exact checks, immutable artifact, observed deployment, user signal, abort decision, and recovery evidence.
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

For Defend the transferred model · Workshop Ci Release Hypothesis Done, challenge the documented misconception with workflow, run, artifact, policy, fault, repair, rollback, and transfer evidence: Pipeline completion is the same as user-value completion.
